echo.description: Display a line of text to stdout. `-n` suppresses the trailing newline, `-e` enables interpretation of backslash escapes, `-E` disables it (default).

printf.description: Write formatted output to stdout. Supports C-style format specifiers: %s (string), %d (integer), %f (float), %x (hex). With `-v`, stores result in variable instead of printing.

read.signature: read [-ers] [-a aname] [-d delim] [-i text] [-n nchars] [-N nchars] [-p prompt] [-t timeout] [-u fd] [name ...]
read.description: Read a line from standard input (or file descriptor) and split it into fields using $IFS. Results are assigned to named variables; excess fields go to the last variable.

cd.description: Change the shell working directory. With no arguments, changes to $HOME. `-L` follows symbolic links (default), `-P` uses the physical directory structure.

pwd.description: Print the absolute pathname of the current working directory. `-L` shows the logical path (default), `-P` resolves symlinks.

export.description: Mark variables and functions for export to child processes. `-n` removes the export property. `-f` exports functions. `-p` lists all exported variables.

source.description: Read and execute commands from filename in the current shell environment. Equivalent to the dot (.) command. Returns the exit status of the last command executed.

alias.description: Define or display aliases. Without arguments, prints all aliases. With a name, prints that alias. With name=value, creates an alias.

unalias.description: Remove alias definitions. `-a` removes all aliases.

type.description: Indicate how each name would be interpreted if used as a command. `-t` prints a single word (alias, keyword, function, builtin, file). `-a` shows all matches.

eval.description: Concatenate all arguments into a single string and execute the result as a shell command in the current environment.

exec.description: Replace the shell with the specified command without creating a new process. If no command is given, redirections take effect in the current shell.

exit.description: Exit the shell with status n. If n is omitted, the exit status is that of the last command executed. Values are modulo 256.

return.description: Return from a shell function with return value n. If n is not supplied, the return value is the exit status of the last command executed in the function.

shift.description: Shift positional parameters to the left by n positions (default 1). Parameter $1 takes the value of $[n+1], and so on. $# is decremented by n.

trap.description: Execute action when the shell receives the specified signal(s). Common signals: EXIT (0), INT (2), TERM (15), ERR, DEBUG, RETURN. `-l` lists signal names. `-p` prints trap commands.

set.description: Set shell options and positional parameters. Common flags: `-e` (exit on error), `-u` (error on unset), `-x` (debug trace), `-o pipefail` (pipe error propagation). `--` separates options from positional parameters.

unset.description: Remove variable or function definitions. `-v` removes variables (default), `-f` removes functions. If neither is given, tries variable first, then function.

declare.description: Declare variables and set attributes. `-i` integer, `-a` indexed array, `-A` associative array, `-r` readonly, `-x` export, `-l` lowercase, `-u` uppercase, `-n` nameref, `-g` global (inside functions).

local.description: Create a local variable within a function. The variable is visible only in the function and its children. Same attributes as declare. Can only be used inside a function.

readonly.description: Mark variables as readonly, preventing reassignment or unsetting. `-a` for arrays, `-A` for associative arrays, `-f` for functions. `-p` displays all readonly variables.

test.description: Evaluate a conditional expression and return 0 (true) or 1 (false). Supports file tests (-f, -d, -e, -r, -w, -x, -s, -L), string tests (-z, -n, =, !=), and integer comparisons (-eq, -ne, -gt, -lt, -ge, -le).

-f.description: True if file exists and is a regular file.

-d.description: True if file exists and is a directory.

-r.description: True if file exists and is readable.

-w.description: True if file exists and is writable.

-x.description: True if file exists and is executable.

-s.description: True if file exists and has size greater than zero.

-L.description: True if file exists and is a symbolic link.

-z.description: True if the length of string is zero.

-n.description: True if the length of string is non-zero.

-eq.description: True if arg1 is equal to arg2 (integer comparison).

-ne.description: True if arg1 is not equal to arg2.

-gt.description: True if arg1 is greater than arg2.

-lt.description: True if arg1 is less than arg2.

-ge.description: True if arg1 is greater than or equal to arg2.

-le.description: True if arg1 is less than or equal to arg2.

[[.description: Extended conditional command. Supports all test operators plus pattern matching (==, =~), logical operators (&&, ||, !), and grouping with parentheses. Word splitting and pathname expansion are not performed.

getopts.description: Parse positional parameters as options. optstring contains expected option letters; a colon after a letter means it requires an argument. Sets OPTARG and OPTIND.

wait.description: Wait for background jobs to complete. Without arguments, waits for all children. With a PID or job spec, waits for that specific process. `-n` waits for the next job to terminate.

jobs.description: Display status of background jobs. `-l` includes PIDs, `-p` shows PIDs only, `-r` shows running only, `-s` shows stopped only.

kill.description: Send a signal to a process or job. Default signal is TERM. Common signals: HUP (1), INT (2), KILL (9), TERM (15), CONT (18), STOP (19).

bg.description: Resume a suspended job in the background, as if it had been started with &.

fg.description: Move a job to the foreground, making it the current job. The shell waits for the job to complete.

pushd.description: Add a directory to the top of the directory stack and change to it. `-n` suppresses the directory change.

popd.description: Remove the top entry from the directory stack and change to the new top. `-n` suppresses the directory change.

mapfile.signature: mapfile [-d delim] [-n count] [-O origin] [-s count] [-t] [-u fd] [-C callback] [-c quantum] [array]
mapfile.description: Read lines from stdin into an indexed array variable. `-t` removes trailing delimiter. `-n count` reads at most count lines. Default array is MAPFILE.

let.description: Evaluate arithmetic expressions. Returns 0 if the last expression evaluates to non-zero, 1 otherwise. Equivalent to ((expression)).

history.description: Display or manipulate the command history list. `-c` clears history, `-d n` deletes entry at offset n, `-a` appends new entries to history file, `-r` reads history file.

complete.signature: complete [-abcdefgjksuv] [-o option] [-A action] [-G globpat] [-W wordlist] [-F function] [-C command] [-X filterpat] [-P prefix] [-S suffix] name [name ...]
complete.description: Specify programmable completion for a command. Defines how arguments to a specific command are completed when the user presses Tab.

compgen.signature: compgen [-abcdefgjksuv] [-o option] [-A action] [-G globpat] [-W wordlist] [-F function] [-C command] [-X filterpat] [-P prefix] [-S suffix] [word]
compgen.description: Generate possible completion matches for word according to the specified options. Used within programmable completion functions.

enable.description: Enable or disable shell builtin commands. `-n` disables a builtin. `-a` lists all builtins showing enabled/disabled status.

shopt.description: Set and unset shell optional behavior. `-s` enables, `-u` disables an option. Common options: extglob, globstar, nullglob, dotglob, nocasematch.

ulimit.description: Get or set user-level resource limits. `-n` for open files, `-u` for max processes, `-v` for virtual memory, `-s` for stack size.

umask.description: Set the file creation mask. The mask is subtracted from the default permissions (777 for dirs, 666 for files). Common: `umask 022` (owner full, others read/execute).

$?.description: The exit status of the most recently executed foreground pipeline. 0 typically indicates success, non-zero indicates failure.

$#.description: The number of positional parameters (arguments) passed to the script or function.

$@.description: All positional parameters as separate words. When double-quoted ("$@"), each parameter expands as a separate word, preserving whitespace.

$*.description: All positional parameters as a single word. When double-quoted ("$*"), expands to a single string with parameters separated by the first character of $IFS.

$$.description: The process ID (PID) of the current shell. In a subshell, it expands to the PID of the parent shell, not the subshell.

$!.description: The process ID of the most recently executed background (asynchronous) command or pipeline.

$0.description: The name of the shell or shell script. Set at shell startup. In a script, it is the filename used to invoke the script.

$_.description: The last argument of the previous command. At shell startup, set to the absolute pathname used to invoke the shell.

$-.description: The current option flags as specified on invocation, by the set builtin, or those set by the shell itself (e.g., -i for interactive).

IFS.description: Internal Field Separator. Used for word splitting after expansion and by read to split lines into fields. Default value is space, tab, newline (<space><tab><newline>).

PATH.description: A colon-separated list of directories in which the shell looks for commands. Searched left to right. Modifying PATH affects which programs the shell can find.

HOME.description: The home directory of the current user. Set automatically at login. Used as the default argument for the cd builtin and for tilde (~) expansion.

PWD.description: The current working directory as set by cd. Updated automatically when the directory changes.

OLDPWD.description: The previous working directory as set by cd. `cd -` changes to $OLDPWD.

SHELL.description: The pathname of the current user's login shell.

USER.description: The name of the current user. Typically set at login.

HOSTNAME.description: The name of the current host. Set automatically at shell startup.

RANDOM.description: Each time this parameter is referenced, it expands to a random integer between 0 and 32767. Assigning a value seeds the random number generator.

SECONDS.description: The number of seconds since the shell was started. If assigned a value, returns the assigned value plus the number of seconds since the assignment.

LINENO.description: The line number in the script or shell function currently executing. Useful for debugging and error messages.

FUNCNAME.description: An array variable containing the names of all shell functions currently in the execution call stack. ${FUNCNAME[0]} is the currently executing function.

BASH_SOURCE.description: An array variable whose members are the source filenames corresponding to the elements in the FUNCNAME array. ${BASH_SOURCE[0]} is the current file.

BASH_LINENO.description: An array of line numbers corresponding to calls in FUNCNAME. ${BASH_LINENO[$i]} is the line number in ${BASH_SOURCE[$i+1]} where ${FUNCNAME[$i]} was called.

PIPESTATUS.description: An array containing the exit statuses of the processes in the most recently executed foreground pipeline. ${PIPESTATUS[0]} is the first command's exit status.

OPTARG.description: Set by getopts to the value of the last option argument processed. Contains the argument when the option requires one.

OPTIND.description: The index of the next argument to be processed by getopts. Initialized to 1 each time the shell or a script is invoked.

REPLY.description: Set by read when no variable names are supplied. Also set by select to the user's input.

BASH_REMATCH.description: An array variable whose members are assigned by the =~ binary operator in [[ ]]. Element 0 is the entire match, elements 1+ are capture group matches.

BASH_VERSION.description: The version number of the current instance of Bash.

EUID.description: The effective user ID of the current user. Read-only. 0 indicates root.

UID.description: The real user ID of the current user. Read-only.

array.description: Indexed arrays hold ordered lists of values. Elements are accessed by integer index starting at 0. Supports ${arr[@]} (all elements), ${#arr[@]} (length), ${!arr[@]} (indices).

[@].description: Expand to all elements of the array as separate words.

[*].description: Expand to all elements as a single word joined by the first character of IFS.

#.description: Return the number of elements in the array.

!.description: Return all indices (keys) of the array.

append.description: Append one or more elements to the end of an indexed array.

slice.description: Extract a slice of the array starting at offset for length elements.

unset_element.description: Remove a specific element from the array.

associative_array.description: Associative arrays use arbitrary strings as keys. Must be declared with `declare -A`. Supports ${map[@]}, ${!map[@]} (keys), ${#map[@]} (size).

!keys.description: Expand to all keys of the associative array.

#size.description: Return the number of key-value pairs.

set.description: Set or update a value for a given key.

string_operations.description: Bash provides extensive string manipulation via parameter expansion. Includes substitution, default values, substring extraction, pattern removal, case modification, and length.

length.description: Length of the string stored in var.

substring.description: Extract substring starting at offset for length characters.

default.description: Use default if var is unset or null.

assign_default.description: Assign default to var if var is unset or null.

alternate.description: Use alternate if var IS set and non-null.

error.description: Display error message and exit if var is unset or null.

prefix_short.description: Remove shortest leading match of pattern.

prefix_long.description: Remove longest leading match of pattern.

suffix_short.description: Remove shortest trailing match of pattern.

suffix_long.description: Remove longest trailing match of pattern.

replace_first.description: Replace the first match of pattern with replacement.

replace_all.description: Replace all matches of pattern with replacement.

arithmetic.signature: $(( expression ))  or  (( expression ))  or  let expression
arithmetic.description: Bash supports integer arithmetic using C-style expressions. Operators: + - * / % ** (power), ++ --, << >> & | ^ ~ (bitwise), && || ! (logical), ternary (?:), comma, and assignment operators (= += -= *= /= %= etc.).

power.description: Exponentiation (a to the power of b).

ternary.description: Ternary conditional: return val1 if condition is non-zero, else val2.

left_shift.description: Left shift a by n bits.

right_shift.description: Right shift a by n bits.

if.signature: if list; then list; [elif list; then list;] ... [else list;] fi
if.description: Execute commands conditionally. The if list is executed; if its exit status is zero, the then list is executed. Otherwise, each elif list is tested in turn.

for.signature: for name [in words ...]; do list; done   or   for (( expr1; expr2; expr3 )); do list; done
for.description: Execute commands for each member in a list. The for-in form iterates over words. The arithmetic form works like C for loops.

while.description: Execute commands repeatedly while a condition is true. The exit status of the while command is the exit status of the last command in list-2, or zero if none executed.

until.description: Execute commands repeatedly until a condition becomes true. Equivalent to `while ! condition; do ... done`.

case.signature: case word in [[(] pattern [| pattern] ...) list ;;] ... esac
case.description: Execute commands matching a pattern. Each pattern is tested in order. `;;` terminates, `;&` falls through, `;;&` tests next pattern.

select.signature: select name [in words ...]; do list; done
select.description: Generate a numbered menu from words. The user's choice is stored in name, the input line in REPLY. Loops until break or EOF.

function.signature: name () compound-command [redirections]   or   function name [()] compound-command [redirections]
function.description: Define a function. Parameters are accessed via $1, $2, etc. Local variables with `local`. Return value via `return n`.
